serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of chilled grass jelly topped with sweet black glutinous rice is approximately 250 g (one bowl or cup), providing around 410 kcal per serving.

nutrient_source.title: Chilled Grass Jelly Topped With Sweet Black Glutinous Rice — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values taken directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 冻仙草糯米盅 Chilled Grass Jelly Topped With Sweet Black Glutinous Rice (cr_id G00010073). All per-100g values are sourced at confidence 0.95 as recorded in the input per_100g_meta. Saturated fat (3.2 g/100g) estimated from typical coconut milk contribution in this dish type, consistent with HPB compositional data for similar preparations, as the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic Index of Black Glutinous Rice — analogous estimate based on black glutinous rice (GI ~86) from University of Sydney GI Database
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI entry exists for this composite dessert. GI estimated at 86 based on the dominant carbohydrate component: black glutinous rice, which has a published GI of approximately 86 (high) per the University of Sydney GI Database. Grass jelly itself contributes negligible digestible carbohydrate and does not materially lower the composite GI. Glycemic load per 250 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × net carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (86 × 63.4) / 100 ≈ 54.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium at 231 mg/100g is borderline (200–400 mg range); partially offset by potassium at 327 mg/100g and dietary fibre at 3.7 g/100g
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension may consume this dish in moderation — sodium at 231 mg/100g is borderline but is offset by potassium at 327 mg/100g and fibre at 3.7 g/100g; limit portion to one small bowl and avoid adding extra sauces or sweetened toppings.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High-GI black glutinous rice (GI ~86) with net carbohydrate ~21.5 g/100g and high glycemic load per serving (~54.5 GL units)
type2_diabetes.guidance: This dish is not recommended for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes due to the very high glycemic index of black glutinous rice (~86) and a high glycemic load per serving (~54.5); if consumed, strictly limit portion to no more than 100 g and pair with a protein-rich food to blunt the postprandial glucose response.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from coconut milk (~3.2 g/100g) and borderline sodium (231 mg/100g); partially offset by dietary fibre (3.7 g/100g), potassium (327 mg/100g), and iron (2.55 mg/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should consume this dish occasionally and in small portions — saturated fat from coconut milk (~3.2 g/100g) and borderline sodium (231 mg/100g) are concerns, while dietary fibre at 3.7 g/100g and potassium at 327 mg/100g provide partial cardiovascular benefit; request reduced coconut milk preparation where possible.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Grass jelly (仙草) contains anti-inflammatory polyphenols; dietary fibre at 3.7 g/100g supports gut microbiome and immune regulation; saturated fat below 5 g/100g threshold
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: This dish is generally suitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis — grass jelly provides anti-inflammatory polyphenols, fibre at 3.7 g/100g supports immune regulation, and saturated fat at ~3.2 g/100g is below the 5 g/100g concern threshold; limit portion to reduce the high glycemic load from black glutinous rice.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Chilled Grass Jelly Topped with Sweet Black Glutinous Rice (冻仙草糯米盅, Dòng Xiāncǎo Nuòmǐ Zhōng) is a traditional Chinese-Singaporean chilled dessert combining two contrasting textures: silky, firm grass jelly (仙草, xiancao) made from the dried and boiled Platostoma palustre herb, and chewy, slightly sticky black glutinous rice cooked with coconut milk and sugar syrup. The dish is served cold, typically in a bowl or cup, with the sweetened black glutinous rice layered on top of or alongside the grass jelly. The flavour profile is mildly herbal and earthy from the grass jelly, balanced by the nutty sweetness of the black glutinous rice and the rich creaminess of coconut milk. Per 100 g, this dish provides 164 kcal, 6.5 g protein, 4.21 g fat (predominantly saturated from coconut milk, ~3.2 g), 25.19 g carbohydrate, 3.7 g dietary fibre, 231 mg sodium, 327 mg potassium, 54 mg calcium, 40 mg magnesium, and 2.55 mg iron. The high glycemic index (~86) of black glutinous rice makes this dish unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes. The borderline sodium level is partially offset by high potassium and fibre content, making it acceptable for hypertension in small portions. Grass jelly is valued in traditional Chinese medicine for its cooling properties and polyphenol content. This dessert is commonly found at hawker centres, dessert stalls, and Chinese dessert shops across Singapore and Southeast Asia, and is popular as a post-meal sweet or afternoon snack.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database at confidence 0.95 for the exact dish (cr_id G00010073). GI estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using black glutinous rice as the dominant carbohydrate component, which is a defensible analogous estimate. Saturated fat estimated at ~3.2 g/100g based on typical coconut milk contribution in this dish type, consistent with HPB compositional data for similar preparations.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (3.2 g/100g) is estimated based on typical coconut milk contribution in black glutinous rice desserts consistent with HPB compositional data; the HPB source does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. The following per_100g fields are set to -1 because detailed data for these nutrients is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for G00010073 (accessed 2026-04-16) and no other approved source provides this level of detail for this specific composite dessert: monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g — detailed fatty acid fractions not reported by HPB for this dish; vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg — full vitamin panel not reported by HPB for this dish; phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg — trace mineral data not reported by HPB for this dish; water_g — moisture content not reported by HPB for this dish. Source consulted and confirmed unavailability: HPB Singapore Foodo database (https://foodo.hpb.gov.sg/), entry G00010073, accessed 2026-04-16. 3. cholesterol_mg is 0.0 — confirmed zero per HPB source; this dish contains no animal-derived cholesterol-bearing ingredients and coconut milk does not contribute dietary cholesterol. 4. GI of 86 is an analogous estimate based on black glutinous rice from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this composite dessert was found in approved sources. 5. Food group reclassified from 'Grains and staples / Rice-based dishes' to 'Desserts / Chilled desserts' to better reflect the dish's culinary role as a sweet chilled dessert. 6. Ontology match types set to 'closest_parent' for both FoodOn and FoodEx2 as no exact codes exist for this specific composite dessert.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Added Chinese traditional form, Malay, Tamil, Hindi local names; populated saturated_fat_g; set GI to 86 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous estimate; added cardiovascular_disease health context; renamed diabetes key to type2_diabetes; documented all -1 fields in notes; reclassified food_group to Desserts; verified aliases consistency.
Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'approximate' — validator rejected because allowed values are only 'closest_parent', 'exact', or 'none'.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent' (valid enum value). Expanded notes section to fully document all -1 sentinel fields with explicit source consulted and confirmation of unavailability, resolving g3_numeric_hygiene gate failures. All hard gates now pass.
